SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper states its main claims clearly and supports them with experiments comparing to JPEG and JPEG 2000 on held-out test images, and provides empirical checks of the continuous relaxation (section 4). The mathematical formulation (quantizer replaced by additive uniform noise, relation to VAEs, loss in (11)) is consistent and derivations appear correct for the assumptions made. There are some simplifying assumptions (independent marginals in code space, piecewise-linear marginal models, limited spatial adaptation in the entropy coder) that are acknowledged and could affect optimality; no formal statistical tests or confidence intervals are reported for empirical comparisons. Overall the technical approach and conclusions are well supported though some methodological limitations and modelling approximations remain and are not fully quantified.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper introduces an end-to-end optimization framework for nonlinear transform coding that jointly trains analysis/synthesis transforms, a marginal entropy model, and uses a continuous uniform-noise relaxation to enable gradient-based training — a novel and nontrivial synthesis of ideas. The use of cascaded GDN/IGDN nonlinearities optimized specifically for rate–distortion, and the explicit connection and distinction to variational autoencoders, are original contributions compared to prior generative or hierarchical compression proposals (discussed in section 3.1 and related work). The technique and perspective would be informative and surprising to experts in image coding and representation learning.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (introduction, method, relation to VAEs, experiments, discussion) and notation is defined with equations referenced explicitly (e.g., (1)–(11)), with an appendix giving architecture and implementation details. Figures and experimental descriptions are informative and the authors openly discuss evaluation subtleties (section 6.3). Some sections are dense and certain implementation choices are described as ad-hoc without exhaustive explanation, so a few passages could be clarified further for full reproducibility.
